# data-anim

> Animate with just HTML. Powered by data attributes. Add a single `<script>` tag, write data attributes, and your HTML comes alive.

## Quick Start

```html
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/data-anim@1/dist/data-anim.min.js"></script>

<h1 data-anim="fadeInUp">Hello, World!</h1>
```

No `init()`. No import. No CSS file. No build step.

## Core Concept

Users add `data-anim` attributes to HTML elements. The library auto-initializes on script load, injects CSS keyframes, and uses IntersectionObserver to trigger animations. No JavaScript knowledge required from the end user.

## Data Attributes

| Attribute | Type | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|---------|-------------|
| `data-anim` | string | (required) | Animation name: fadeIn, fadeInUp, fadeInDown, fadeInLeft, fadeInRight, fadeOut, slideInUp, slideInDown, slideInLeft, slideInRight, zoomIn, zoomOut, zoomInUp, zoomInDown, bounce, bounceIn, bounceInUp, bounceInDown, shake, pulse, wobble, flip, swing, rubberBand, rotateIn, rotateInDownLeft, rotateInDownRight, blur, clipReveal, typewriter |
| `data-anim-trigger` | string | "scroll" | Trigger type: scroll, load, click, hover |
| `data-anim-duration` | number | 500 | Duration in ms |
| `data-anim-delay` | number | 0 | Delay in ms |
| `data-anim-easing` | string | "ease" | CSS easing or preset: ease, ease-out-expo, ease-out-back, spring |
| `data-anim-once` | boolean | false | Play animation only once |
| `data-anim-offset` | number | 0.2 | Scroll trigger threshold (0-1) |
| `data-anim-mirror` | boolean | false | Reverse animation on scroll out |
| `data-anim-distance` | number | 30 | Translation distance in px |
| `data-anim-scale` | number | 0.9 | Scale factor for zoom animations |
| `data-anim-fill` | string | "both" | CSS animation-fill-mode |
| `data-anim-iteration` | number | 1 | CSS animation-iteration-count |
| `data-anim-stagger` | number | — | Parent attr: delay between children (ms) |
| `data-anim-stagger-from` | string | "start" | Stagger direction: start, end, center, edges |
| `data-anim-disable` | string | — | Disable at breakpoint: mobile, tablet, desktop, or px value |
| `data-anim-mobile` | string | — | Override animation name on mobile |
| `data-anim-anchor` | string | — | CSS selector of anchor element for scroll trigger |

## Built-in Animations (30)

**Fade (6):** fadeIn, fadeOut, fadeInUp, fadeInDown, fadeInLeft, fadeInRight
**Slide (4):** slideInUp, slideInDown, slideInLeft, slideInRight
**Zoom (4):** zoomIn, zoomOut, zoomInUp, zoomInDown
**Bounce (4):** bounce, bounceIn, bounceInUp, bounceInDown
**Attention (6):** shake, pulse, wobble, flip, swing, rubberBand
**Rotate (3):** rotateIn, rotateInDownLeft, rotateInDownRight
**Special (3):** blur, clipReveal, typewriter

## Triggers

- **scroll** (default): IntersectionObserver-based. Animates when element enters viewport.
- **load**: Fires on DOMContentLoaded. Use `data-anim-delay` to stagger.
- **click**: Replays animation on every click. Uses animation reset trick (reflow).
- **hover**: Plays on mouseenter, resets on mouseleave. Also responds to focus-visible. Elements with hover trigger are NOT hidden initially.

## Stagger

Apply `data-anim-stagger` to a parent element. All direct children with `data-anim` get incremental delays.

```html
<ul data-anim-stagger="100" data-anim-stagger-from="center">
  <li data-anim="fadeInUp">0ms from center</li>
  <li data-anim="fadeInUp">100ms from center</li>
  <li data-anim="fadeInUp">200ms from center</li>
</ul>
```

## Anti-FOUC (Flash of Unstyled Content)

Three-layer defense:
1. **Inline CSS injection**: Script immediately injects `opacity: 0` on `[data-anim]` elements before paint.
2. **`<noscript>` fallback**: Auto-injects noscript block to show content if JS fails.
3. **5-second timeout**: Forces all hidden elements visible if initialization stalls.

Above-fold elements get a fast-reveal (150ms) instead of full animation to prevent perceived blinking.

## Accessibility

- `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` automatically disables all animations.
- Hover triggers also respond to `focus-visible` for keyboard users.
- No content reflow or position changes that confuse screen readers.

## Technical Details

- **CSS Animations only** (no Web Animations API)
- **GPU-accelerated**: Only animates transform + opacity (exceptions: blur uses filter, clipReveal uses clip-path)
- **IntersectionObserver** for scroll detection (no scroll event listeners)
- **MutationObserver** for dynamic elements (no manual refresh needed)
- **Bundle size**: ~2.8KB gzip, 0 dependencies
- **Browser support**: All modern browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)

## Installation

**CDN:**
```html
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/data-anim@1/dist/data-anim.min.js"></script>
```

**npm:**
```bash
npm install data-anim
```

```js
import 'data-anim'; // auto-initializes
```

## Easing Presets

| Name | cubic-bezier |
|------|-------------|
| ease | (0.25, 0.1, 0.25, 1) |
| ease-out-expo | (0.16, 1, 0.3, 1) |
| ease-out-back | (0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1) |
| spring | (0.175, 0.885, 0.32, 1.275) |

## Common Patterns

**Hero with staggered load animation:**
```html
<h1 data-anim="fadeIn" data-anim-trigger="load">Title</h1>
<p data-anim="fadeInUp" data-anim-trigger="load" data-anim-delay="200">Subtitle</p>
<button data-anim="fadeInUp" data-anim-trigger="load" data-anim-delay="400">CTA</button>
```

**Card grid with stagger:**
```html
<div data-anim-stagger="100" data-anim-stagger-from="center">
  <div data-anim="fadeInUp" data-anim-once>Card 1</div>
  <div data-anim="fadeInUp" data-anim-once>Card 2</div>
  <div data-anim="fadeInUp" data-anim-once>Card 3</div>
</div>
```

**Mobile-optimized animation:**
```html
<div data-anim="slideInLeft" data-anim-mobile="fadeIn">
  Desktop: slide in from left. Mobile: simple fade.
</div>
```

**Interactive button:**
```html
<button data-anim="bounce" data-anim-trigger="click">Click me!</button>
```

**Hover effect:**
```html
<div data-anim="pulse" data-anim-trigger="hover">Hover me</div>
```
